title: To eliminate certain limitations and exclusions regarding defense articles and requirements regarding security assistance and sales with respect to the Republic of Cyprus.
plain_summary: This bill removes special rules that made it harder for the United States to sell weapons and provide military help to Cyprus, Greece, and Turkey. Right now, the President has to prove to Congress every year that these countries are doing certain things before the US can help them with defense. For Cyprus, the President has to show they are fighting money laundering and not helping Russian military ships. For Greece and Turkey, the President has to prove that giving them weapons will keep peace in the area. This bill gets rid of all those extra requirements so the US can treat these countries like other allies.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Section 1  Elimination of Requirements Concerning the Republic of Cyprus
sections.text: This section removes certain rules and restrictions about selling weapons and providing military help to Cyprus. First, it changes a previous law from two thousand twenty called the National Defense Authorization Act. The changes remove parts of that law that created limits on what defense equipment could be sold to Cyprus. The bill also removes requirements that Congress had to follow when providing security assistance and making certain sales to Cyprus. Second, it changes the Foreign Assistance Act of nineteen sixty-one by removing two specific subsections that created additional requirements about helping Cyprus with security matters and weapons sales.
